reference: the history of artificial intelligence as a field of study began in the middle of the twentieth century when researchers first proposed that machines could be made to simulate aspects of human reasoning learning and decision making and over the decades that followed the field went through cycles of optimism and disappointment before the deep learning revolution finally delivered systems that could see hear and converse at superhuman levels in narrow domains

climate change represents one of the most pressing challenges facing humanity today and addressing it will require unprecedented coordination across governments industries and individuals to reduce greenhouse gas emissions transition to renewable energy adapt agriculture protect biodiversity and build resilient infrastructure capable of withstanding the more extreme weather patterns that scientists project for the coming decades

the practice of writing software has evolved dramatically since the early days of punched cards and assembly language and modern developers now work with high level languages rich tooling distributed version control automated testing continuous integration cloud infrastructure containerisation and increasingly artificial intelligence assistants that can suggest complete and even refactor entire blocks of code in real time
